records.ety: [Prob. of same origin as blear. See Blear.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Blurred (blrd); p. pr. & vb. n. Blurring.]

defs: To render obscure by making the form or outline of confused and uncertain, as by soiling; to smear; to make indistinct and confused; as, to blur manuscript by handling it while damp; to blur the impression of a woodcut by an excess of ink.
quotes: But time hath nothing blurred those lines of favor Which then he wore.

defs: To cause imperfection of vision in; to dim; to darken.
quotes: Her eyes are blurred with the lightning's glare.

defs: To sully; to stain; to blemish, as reputation.
quotes: Sarcasms may eclipse thine own, But can not blur my lost renown.

defs: That which obscures without effacing; a stain; a blot, as upon paper or other substance.
quotes: As for those who cleanse blurs with blotted fingers, they make it worse.

defs: A dim, confused appearance; indistinctness of vision; as, to see things with a blur; it was all blur.

quotes: Lest she . . . will with her railing set a great blur on mine honesty and good name.
